neutral.summary: A bipartisan border security bill passed the House committee, allocating funds for technology upgrades and additional personnel at border crossings.

left.take: Security is important, but this bill ignores the humanitarian crisis. We need comprehensive immigration reform, not just more enforcement.

quotes.text: We can secure our borders while treating people with dignity and compassion.
quotes.speaker: Rep. Carlos Rivera

quotes.text: This bill is just more walls and surveillance. Where's the pathway to citizenship for dreamers?
quotes.speaker: Maya Patel, Immigration Rights Advocate

quotes.text: Militarizing the border doesn't address the root causes driving migration - poverty, violence, and climate change.
quotes.speaker: Dr. Antonio Gomez, Migration Studies

quotes.text: We're spending billions on enforcement while ignoring the economic contributions of immigrants.
quotes.speaker: Sen. Rachel Cohen

right.take: Finally, some common sense! Secure borders are not optional - every nation has the right to control who enters and enforce its laws.

quotes.text: Border security is national security. This bill is long overdue.
quotes.speaker: Rep. Jennifer Walsh

quotes.text: Every country has borders. Every country has the right to enforce them. This should not be controversial.
quotes.speaker: Gov. Tom Anderson

quotes.text: Legal immigration is great. Illegal immigration is not. It's that simple.
quotes.speaker: Sheriff Mike Thompson

quotes.text: We cannot have open borders and a welfare state. You pick one.
quotes.speaker: Policy Analyst David Brooks

quotes.text: The American people have been clear: they want secure borders. We're finally delivering.
quotes.speaker: Sen. Mark Johnson
